purpose: Reconcile decision-critical valuation inputs to primary evidence without treating analyst ranges as reported facts.

primary_sources.title: NVR 2025 Form 10-K filing index and XBRL exhibits

resolved_facts: 2025 net income was $1.340 billion, diluted EPS was $436.55, and diluted weighted-average shares were 3.069 million.

2025 operating cash flow was $1.121 billion and property, plant and equipment purchases were $24.508 million; operating cash flow benefited from a $335.147 million inventory decrease and absorbed a $200.657 million increase in contract-land deposits and $238.260 million of net mortgage-loan activity.

At year-end 2025 NVR controlled about 169,250 finished lots under LPAs using $920.1 million of cash deposits and $4.6 million of letters of credit. It also controlled contracts expected to yield about 38,200 additional lots with $42.3 million of deposits, $9.0 million refundable.

Gross contract-land deposits were $962.416 million, the allowance was $110.958 million, and the net asset was $851.458 million. 2025 pre-tax deposit impairment charges were $75.9 million versus $7.2 million in 2024.
The normal LPA legal loss is limited to the deposit, subject to very limited specific-performance cases; land developers generally finance and control development without recourse to NVR.
Existing LPAs could require about $733.9 million of additional deposits if developer milestones are met and NVR exercises the purchase options, mostly within three years.

At year-end, the homebuilding and mortgage businesses held approximately $1.916 billion of cash in aggregate; senior notes were approximately $909 million. The revolver and mortgage repurchase facility had no borrowings.

gap_assessments.reconciliation: Reported earnings, cash flow, capex, working-capital movements, backlog and cancellation data create a reproducible 2025 anchor. They do not establish a full-cycle community-level margin, volume, cancellation and working-capital distribution. The valuation's $3,300/$5,600/$8,000 per-share range remains an analyst normalization, not a filing-derived bridge.
gap_assessments.overlap_control: The separate lot-option component may include only value above the lot economics required to support normalized homebuilding earnings; deposits and related impairments cannot be counted in both components.
gap_assessments.falsifier: A sustained fall in settlements and gross margin accompanied by rising cancellations, deposit impairments and cash absorption would invalidate the base owner-earnings normalization.
gap_assessments.monitoring_source: Quarterly and annual SEC filings: settlements, gross margin, backlog, cancellation rate, inventory, contract-land deposits, impairments and operating cash flow.
gap_assessments.unresolved: A community/cohort history sufficient to reproduce low/base/high normalized owner earnings across at least one housing downturn is not disclosed in the current packet.

gap_assessments.reconciliation: Quantity, deposits, legal downside, impairment allowance, additional-deposit commitments and limited JV/owned-land exposures are reconciled. Opening cadence, lot-level purchase prices, community economics and probability of exercise are not disclosed, so the $200/$600/$1,200 per-share option range cannot yet be independently reproduced.
gap_assessments.overlap_control: Only incremental option value after deposit carrying value, expected abandonment loss and the lots consumed in the normalized earnings forecast is additive.
gap_assessments.falsifier: Deposit impairment charges remain materially above historical levels or controlled-lot growth fails to translate into profitable community openings.
gap_assessments.monitoring_source: Form 10-K Note 3 and quarterly filings for controlled lots, cash deposits, allowance, impairment charges and commitments.
gap_assessments.unresolved: No primary lot-by-lot or community opening schedule exists in the packet.

gap_assessments.reconciliation: Gross cash, debt, undrawn facilities and near-term deposit commitments are known. The valuation appropriately credits only a fraction of gross net cash, but the $250/$350/$450 per-share surplus range lacks a disclosed minimum homebuilding, mortgage and stress-liquidity policy.
gap_assessments.overlap_control: Cash pledged, restricted, required for mortgage funding, necessary for deposits and working capital, or needed to survive the cycle is not surplus.
gap_assessments.falsifier: Cash falls below modeled operating and deposit needs or debt/facility usage rises during an ordinary slowdown.
gap_assessments.monitoring_source: Quarterly balance sheet, cash-flow statement, debt note, mortgage funding note and LPA commitments.
gap_assessments.unresolved: Management does not disclose a decision-useful minimum liquidity requirement or stress cash budget.

committee_conclusion: Primary evidence materially improves the factual base but does not reproduce the valuation ranges. Keep the decision evidence-blocked and require raters to abstain from approval.
